web_text:
The first book I wrote, Theophilus, concerned all that Jesus began both to do and to teach, until the day in which he was received up, after he had given commandment through the Holy Spirit to the apostles whom he had chosen. To these he also showed himself alive after he suffered, by many proofs, appearing to them over a period of forty days, and speaking about God’s Kingdom. Being assembled together with them, he commanded them,
“Don’t depart from Jerusalem, but wait for the promise of the Father, which you heard from me.
slang_text:
So, Theophilus, my main dude, that first scroll I cranked out covered everything Jesus got busy doing and teaching, all the way up to the day he got hoisted up to heaven -- but not before he laid some serious commandments on the apostles he'd picked out, straight from the Holy Spirit. After he went through all that suffering, he showed up alive again and proved it, hella times, over like forty whole days, kicking it with the crew and rapping about God's Kingdom. One time when they were all hanging together, he told them straight up,
"Don't you dare bounce outta Jerusalem. Chill and wait on that promise from the Father, the one you already heard me talkin' about.

web_text:
For John indeed baptized in water, but you will be baptized in the Holy Spirit not many days from now.” Therefore when they had come together, they asked him, “Lord, are you now restoring the kingdom to Israel?” He said to them,
“It isn’t for you to know times or seasons which the Father has set within his own authority. But you will receive power when the Holy Spirit has come upon you. You will be witnesses to me in Jerusalem, in all Judea and Samaria, and to the uttermost parts of the earth.”
slang_text:
'Cause John dunked people in water, sure, but pretty soon you're gonna get baptized in the Holy Spirit -- no lie, it's coming way before long." So when they were all together, they asked him, "Lord, is this the big moment? You finally bringing the kingdom back to Israel?" He came back with,
"It ain't your business to know the times or seasons -- that's the Father's call, totally his own authority. But you're gonna get power when the Holy Spirit lands on you, and you'll be my witnesses, tellin' it all over Jerusalem, all through Judea and Samaria, and clear out to the ends of the earth, fer sure."

web_text: When he had said these things, as they were looking, he was taken up, and a cloud received him out of their sight. While they were looking steadfastly into the sky as he went, behold, two men stood by them in white clothing, who also said, “You men of Galilee, why do you stand looking into the sky? This Jesus, who was received up from you into the sky, will come back in the same way as you saw him going into the sky.” Then they returned to Jerusalem from the mountain called Olivet, which is near Jerusalem, a Sabbath day’s journey away.
slang_text: Soon as he said all that, right while they were staring, he got lifted straight up, and a cloud rolled in and swallowed him outta sight -- totally epic. While they were still gawking up at the sky, tryin' to track him, boom, two dudes in blindingly white clothes just showed up next to 'em and said, "Yo, you Galilee guys, why you standin' around staring at the sky? This same Jesus, the one who just got taken up from you into the sky, he's comin' back the exact same way you watched him go, no doubt about it." Then they bounced back to Jerusalem from the mountain they call Olivet, which is just a Sabbath day's walk from the city.

web_text: When they had come in, they went up into the upper room, where they were staying; that is Peter, John, James, Andrew, Philip, Thomas, Bartholomew, Matthew, James the son of Alphaeus, Simon the Zealot, and Judas the son of James. All these with one accord continued steadfastly in prayer and supplication, along with the women, and Mary the mother of Jesus, and with his brothers. In these days, Peter stood up in the middle of the disciples (and the number of names was about one hundred twenty), and said, “Brothers, it was necessary that this Scripture should be fulfilled, which the Holy Spirit spoke before by the mouth of David concerning Judas, who was guide to those who took Jesus.
slang_text: Once they got back inside, they headed up to the upper room where they'd been crashing -- that's Peter, John, James, Andrew, Philip, Thomas, Bartholomew, Matthew, James the son of Alphaeus, Simon the Zealot, and Judas the son of James. All these guys, totally united, kept steady in prayer, along with the women, Mary the mother of Jesus, and his brothers too. Around this time, Peter got up in front of the whole crew (about one hundred twenty names, all told) and said, "Brothers, this Scripture had to go down just like it says, the stuff the Holy Spirit called ahead of time through David's mouth about Judas, the guy who led the mob that grabbed Jesus.

web_text:
For he was counted with us, and received his portion in this ministry. Now this man obtained a field with the reward for his wickedness, and falling headlong, his body burst open, and all his intestines gushed out. It became known to everyone who lived in Jerusalem that in their language that field was called ‘Akeldama,’ that is, ‘The field of blood.’ For it is written in the book of Psalms,
‘Let his habitation be made desolate.
Let no one dwell therein;’
and,
‘Let another take his office.’
slang_text:
See, he was counted right in with us, got his own slice of this ministry gig. But then this dude bought himself a field with the payout from his bogus, dirty deed, and he took a header, busted wide open, guts and all -- way harsh, straight-up grody. Everybody in Jerusalem heard about it, so in their own language they started calling that field 'Akeldama,' which means 'The Field of Blood.' 'Cause it's written right there in the book of Psalms,
'Let his place end up deserted,
Let nobody live there';
and also,
'Let somebody else take his job.'

web_text: “Of the men therefore who have accompanied us all the time that the Lord Jesus went in and out among us, beginning from the baptism of John, to the day that he was received up from us, of these one must become a witness with us of his resurrection.” They put forward two, Joseph called Barsabbas, who was also called Justus, and Matthias. They prayed, and said, “You, Lord, who know the hearts of all men, show which one of these two you have chosen
slang_text: "So outta all the guys who rolled with us the whole time the Lord Jesus was in and out among us, startin' from when John baptized him clear up to the day he got taken up outta our sight -- one of these guys has gotta step up and be a witness with us to his resurrection." They put two names on the table, Joseph (they called him Barsabbas, also went by Justus) and Matthias. Then they prayed, "You, Lord, you know what's goin' on in everybody's heart -- show us straight up which of these two you picked

web_text: to take part in this ministry and apostleship from which Judas fell away, that he might go to his own place.” They drew lots for them, and the lot fell on Matthias, and he was counted with the eleven apostles.
slang_text: to take up this ministry and apostle gig that Judas ditched when he bailed off to his own place." So they threw the dice, so to speak, and the lot landed on Matthias -- and just like that, he got counted right in with the eleven apostles. Cowabunga.

web_text: Now when the day of Pentecost had come, they were all with one accord in one place. Suddenly there came from the sky a sound like the rushing of a mighty wind, and it filled all the house where they were sitting. Tongues like fire appeared and were distributed to them, and one sat on each of them. They were all filled with the Holy Spirit, and began to speak with other languages, as the Spirit gave them the ability to speak.
slang_text: So the day of Pentecost finally rolls around, and the whole crew's packed into one spot, totally tight, all on the same page. Then outta nowhere -- bam -- this sound comes ripping down from the sky, like a hurricane-force wind, and it fills up the entire house they're chillin' in. Way gnarly. Then these little tongues of fire show up, split up, and land one on each of their heads. Everybody gets totally jacked full of the Holy Spirit and starts busting out in other languages they never even learned, 'cause the Spirit's giving them the words. Totally psychedelic.

web_text: Now there were dwelling in Jerusalem Jews, devout men, from every nation under the sky. When this sound was heard, the multitude came together, and were bewildered, because everyone heard them speaking in his own language. They were all amazed and marveled, saying to one another, “Behold, aren’t all these who speak Galileans? How do we hear, everyone in our own native language?
slang_text: Now Jerusalem's packed with devout Jewish dudes visiting from literally every nation under the sun. When they heard that racket, the whole crowd came running, totally trippin', 'cause every single one of them heard the disciples talking in their own native tongue. Everybody's minds are blown, going, "Whoa, hold up -- aren't all these guys Galileans? So how come we're each hearing our own language?

web_text: Parthians, Medes, Elamites, and people from Mesopotamia, Judea, Cappadocia, Pontus, Asia, Phrygia, Pamphylia, Egypt, the parts of Libya around Cyrene, visitors from Rome, both Jews and proselytes, Cretans and Arabians: we hear them speaking in our languages the mighty works of God!” They were all amazed, and were perplexed, saying to one another, “What does this mean?”
slang_text: Parthians, Medes, Elamites, folks from Mesopotamia, Judea, Cappadocia, Pontus, Asia, Phrygia, Pamphylia, Egypt, the Libya region near Cyrene, tourists from Rome (Jews and converts alike), Cretans, Arabians -- we're all hearing them, in our own languages, going off about the mighty stuff God's done!" Everybody's amazed, totally bewildered, asking each other, "So what's the deal here? What's going on?"

web_text: Others, mocking, said, “They are filled with new wine.” But Peter, standing up with the eleven, lifted up his voice, and spoke out to them, “You men of Judea, and all you who dwell at Jerusalem, let this be known to you, and listen to my words. For these aren’t drunken, as you suppose, seeing it is only the third hour of the day. But this is what has been spoken through the prophet Joel:
slang_text: But some clowns in the crowd just started cracking jokes, going, "Pfft, these guys are just wasted on new wine." No way, dudes. So Peter stands up with the other eleven, raises his voice, and lays it out straight: "Men of Judea, and everybody chillin' here in Jerusalem, listen up, let me set you straight. These folks aren't hammered like y'all are thinking -- it's only nine in the morning, give me a break. Nah, this is exactly what the prophet Joel called it, way back when:

web_text:
‘It will be in the last days, says God,
that I will pour out my Spirit on all flesh.
Your sons and your daughters will prophesy.
Your young men will see visions.
Your old men will dream dreams. Yes, and on my servants and on my handmaidens in those days,
I will pour out my Spirit, and they will prophesy. I will show wonders in the sky above,
and signs on the earth beneath;
blood, and fire, and billows of smoke. The sun will be turned into darkness,
and the moon into blood,
before the great and glorious day of the Lord comes.
slang_text:
'It's gonna go down in the last days, says God,
that I'm gonna pour out my Spirit on every last one of you.
Your sons and your daughters are gonna prophesy.
Your young dudes are gonna see visions.
Your old-timers are gonna dream righteous dreams. Yeah, and on my servants, dudes and dudettes both,
in those days I'm gonna pour out my Spirit, and they're gonna prophesy. I'll show wicked wonders up in the sky,
and signs down here on the earth --
blood, and fire, and clouds of smoke billowing up. The sun's gonna go dark as night,
and the moon's gonna turn blood-red,
before that great and glorious day of the Lord shows up.

web_text: It will be that whoever will call on the name of the Lord will be saved.’ “Men of Israel, hear these words! Jesus of Nazareth, a man approved by God to you by mighty works and wonders and signs which God did by him among you, even as you yourselves know, him, being delivered up by the determined counsel and foreknowledge of God, you have taken by the hand of lawless men, crucified and killed; whom God raised up, having freed him from the agony of death, because it was not possible that he should be held by it.
slang_text: Whoever calls on the name of the Lord is gonna get saved, fer sure.' "Men of Israel, listen up! Jesus of Nazareth was a dude God stamped with approval right in front of you -- through mighty works, wonders, and signs God pulled off through him, right in your own backyard, and y'all know it's true. This same Jesus got handed over, exactly like God planned it out ahead of time, no surprise to Him. And you guys let some lawless dudes nail him to a cross and kill him. But God raised him back up, busted him loose from the grip of death, 'cause there was no way death was ever gonna hold onto him. That's just how it had to go down.

web_text:
For David says concerning him,
‘I saw the Lord always before my face,
For he is on my right hand, that I should not be moved. Therefore my heart was glad, and my tongue rejoiced.
Moreover my flesh also will dwell in hope; because you will not leave my soul in Hades,
neither will you allow your Holy One to see decay. You made known to me the ways of life.
You will make me full of gladness with your presence.’
slang_text:
'I keep the Lord right in front of my face, always,
'cause He's right there at my right hand, so nothing's gonna knock me down. That's why my heart's stoked, and my tongue's shoutin' for joy.
And get this -- even my body's gonna kick it in hope, 'cause you won't leave my soul stuck in Hades,
and you won't let your Holy One rot away, no way. You showed me the paths of real life.
You're gonna fill me up totally with gladness just by being near.'

web_text: “Brothers, I may tell you freely of the patriarch David, that he both died and was buried, and his tomb is with us to this day. Therefore, being a prophet, and knowing that God had sworn with an oath to him that of the fruit of his body, according to the flesh, he would raise up the Christ to sit on his throne, he foreseeing this spoke about the resurrection of the Christ, that his soul wasn’t left in Hades, and his flesh didn’t see decay. This Jesus God raised up, to which we all are witnesses.
slang_text: "Brothers, I can tell you straight up about our main man David -- he died, got buried, and his tomb's still sitting right here with us today, no lie. But since he was a prophet, and he knew God had sworn an oath to him that one of his own descendants would sit on his throne as the Christ, David was actually looking ahead and talking about the Christ's resurrection -- that his soul wouldn't get stuck in Hades, and his body wouldn't rot. And that's exactly what happened to this Jesus -- God raised him up, and every single one of us saw it go down with our own eyes.

web_text:
Being therefore exalted by the right hand of God, and having received from the Father the promise of the Holy Spirit, he has poured out this, which you now see and hear. For David didn’t ascend into the heavens, but he says himself,
‘The Lord said to my Lord, “Sit by my right hand, until I make your enemies a footstool for your feet.”’ “Let all the house of Israel therefore know certainly that God has made him both Lord and Christ, this Jesus whom you crucified.”
slang_text: 'The Lord said to my Lord, "Kick it right here at my right hand, till I stack your enemies up like a footstool under your feet."' "So let the whole house of Israel know this for real, no doubt about it -- God has made this same Jesus, the one you nailed to the cross, both Lord and Christ. Righteous and true."

web_text: Now when they heard this, they were cut to the heart, and said to Peter and the rest of the apostles, “Brothers, what shall we do?” Peter said to them, “Repent, and be baptized, every one of you, in the name of Jesus Christ for the forgiveness of sins, and you will receive the gift of the Holy Spirit. For the promise is to you, and to your children, and to all who are far off, even as many as the Lord our God will call to himself.” With many other words he testified, and exhorted them, saying, “Save yourselves from this crooked generation!”
slang_text: When the crowd heard all this, it straight-up wrecked them inside, and they said to Peter and the rest of the apostles, "Brothers, what are we supposed to do?" Peter told 'em, "Turn it around, every last one of you, and get baptized in the name of Jesus Christ so your sins get wiped clean, and you'll get the Holy Spirit as a gift. This promise is for you, for your kids, and for everybody way out there too -- as many as the Lord our God calls to himself." He kept laying it on with a ton more words, begging them, "Save yourselves from this bogus, crooked generation!"

web_text: Then those who gladly received his word were baptized. There were added that day about three thousand souls. They continued steadfastly in the apostles’ teaching and fellowship, in the breaking of bread, and prayer. Fear came on every soul, and many wonders and signs were done through the apostles. All who believed were together, and had all things in common.
slang_text: So everybody who totally took his word to heart got baptized right then. About three thousand souls got added to the crew that same day -- epic. They stuck like glue to the apostles' teaching and hanging out together, breaking bread and praying nonstop. A serious awe fell over everybody, and the apostles were pulling off all kinds of wicked wonders and signs. Every believer stuck together and shared everything they had, no questions asked.

web_text: They sold their possessions and goods, and distributed them to all, according as anyone had need. Day by day, continuing steadfastly with one accord in the temple, and breaking bread at home, they took their food with gladness and singleness of heart, praising God, and having favor with all the people. The Lord added to the assembly day by day those who were being saved.
slang_text: They sold off their stuff and their goods and handed the cash out to whoever needed it, for real, no holding back. Day after day, they kept rolling into the temple together, totally tight-knit, then breaking bread back at their houses, chowing down with straight-up joy and pure hearts, praising God the whole time, and everybody in town thought they were choice people. And the Lord kept adding more and more folks who were getting saved to the group, day after day.

web_text: Peter and John were going up into the temple at the hour of prayer, the ninth hour. A certain man who was lame from his mother’s womb was being carried, whom they laid daily at the door of the temple which is called Beautiful, to ask gifts for the needy of those who entered into the temple. Seeing Peter and John about to go into the temple, he asked to receive gifts for the needy. Peter, fastening his eyes on him, with John, said, “Look at us.”
slang_text: Peter and John were cruisin' up to the temple for prayer time, three o'clock in the afternoon. There was this dude who'd been lame since he popped outta the womb, and his buddies hauled him every single day to plant him at the gate called Beautiful, so he could beg spare change off folks headed into the temple. Grody as it sounds, that was his whole gig. So he clocks Peter and John about to walk in and hits 'em up for a handout. Peter locks eyes on him, John right there too, and goes, "Yo, check us out."

web_text: He listened to them, expecting to receive something from them. But Peter said, “I have no silver or gold, but what I have, that I give you. In the name of Jesus Christ of Nazareth, get up and walk!” He took him by the right hand, and raised him up. Immediately his feet and his ankle bones received strength. Leaping up, he stood, and began to walk. He entered with them into the temple, walking, leaping, and praising God.
slang_text: So the guy locks in on 'em, totally expecting some spare change to drop. But Peter goes, "Dude, I ain't got no silver, no gold, zip -- but what I DO got, I'm handin' it over. In the name of Jesus Christ of Nazareth, get up and walk!" He grabs the guy's right hand and hauls him up, and bam -- his feet and ankles get totally jacked with strength, right then and there. Guy jumps up, standing there, starts walking around like it's no big deal. Then he rolls into the temple with 'em, walking, jumping, praising God like it's the most epic thing that ever happened -- 'cause it was.

web_text: All the people saw him walking and praising God. They recognized him, that it was he who used to sit begging for gifts for the needy at the Beautiful Gate of the temple. They were filled with wonder and amazement at what had happened to him. As the lame man who was healed held on to Peter and John, all the people ran together to them in the porch that is called Solomon’s, greatly wondering. When Peter saw it, he responded to the people, “You men of Israel, why do you marvel at this man? Why do you fasten your eyes on us, as though by our own power or godliness we had made him walk?
slang_text: Everybody saw him walking around, praising God, and their jaws hit the floor -- they recognized him as the same dude who used to just sit there beggin' at the Beautiful Gate. They were straight-up trippin' on what happened to him, totally blown away. And since the healed guy wouldn't let go of Peter and John, this whole crowd comes running up to 'em at Solomon's Porch, gawking like crazy. Peter clocks the scene and goes off on the crowd, "Yo, men of Israel, what's your damage? Why you all trippin' over this guy? Why you starin' at us like me and John pulled this off with our own righteous power or holiness?

web_text: The God of Abraham, Isaac, and Jacob, the God of our fathers, has glorified his Servant Jesus, whom you delivered up, and denied in the presence of Pilate, when he had determined to release him. But you denied the Holy and Righteous One, and asked for a murderer to be granted to you, and killed the Prince of life, whom God raised from the dead, to which we are witnesses. By faith in his name, his name has made this man strong, whom you see and know. Yes, the faith which is through him has given him this perfect soundness in the presence of you all.
slang_text: The God of Abraham, Isaac, and Jacob, the God of our own fathers -- he's the one who made his Servant Jesus totally righteous, and you guys are the ones who handed him over and dissed him right in front of Pilate, even after Pilate had already decided to cut him loose. That's bogus. You dogged the Holy and Righteous One and begged for some murderer to get sprung instead, and you flat-out killed the Prince of life -- but God raised him from the dead, and we saw it with our own eyes, no lie. It's faith in his name that's made this dude strong, the same guy you're all lookin' at and know for real. Yeah, the faith that comes through Jesus is what gave him this perfect, totally healed body, right here in front of all of you.

web_text: “Now, brothers, I know that you did this in ignorance, as did also your rulers. But the things which God announced by the mouth of all his prophets, that Christ should suffer, he thus fulfilled. “Repent therefore, and turn again, that your sins may be blotted out, so that there may come times of refreshing from the presence of the Lord, and that he may send Christ Jesus, who was ordained for you before,
slang_text: "Now look, brothers, I get it -- you didn't have a clue what you were doin', and neither did your rulers. But everything God laid out through the mouths of all his prophets, that the Christ had to suffer, that's exactly how it went down, right on schedule. So repent, turn yourselves around, get a clue, so your sins can get totally wiped clean, and so times of refreshing can roll in straight from the presence of the Lord, and so he can send Christ Jesus, the one already picked out for you way back when,

web_text: whom heaven must receive until the times of restoration of all things, which God spoke long ago by the mouth of his holy prophets. For Moses indeed said to the fathers, ‘The Lord God will raise up a prophet for you from among your brothers, like me. You shall listen to him in all things whatever he says to you. It will be that every soul that will not listen to that prophet will be utterly destroyed from among the people.’ Yes, and all the prophets from Samuel and those who followed after, as many as have spoken, they also told of these days.
slang_text: the same Jesus heaven's gotta hold onto until everything gets totally restored, just like God said way back through his holy prophets' mouths. 'Cause Moses himself told the fathers, 'The Lord God's gonna raise up a prophet for you guys from among your own brothers, someone like me. You better listen to him, whatever he tells you -- no talkin' to the hand on this one. And any soul that won't listen to that prophet is gonna get totally wiped out from the people, for real.' And yeah, all the prophets, goin' all the way back to Samuel and everybody after him, every last one who spoke up, they were all callin' out these very days too.

web_text: You are the children of the prophets, and of the covenant which God made with our fathers, saying to Abraham, ‘In your offspring will all the families of the earth be blessed.’ God, having raised up his servant Jesus, sent him to you first to bless you, in turning away every one of you from your wickedness.”
slang_text: You guys are the kids of the prophets, and you're in on the covenant God cut with our fathers, when he told Abraham, 'Through your family line, every single family on the whole earth's gonna get blessed.' God raised up his servant Jesus and sent him to you first, dudes, to bless you, by turning every last one of you away from your grody, wicked ways."

web_text: As they spoke to the people, the priests and the captain of the temple and the Sadducees came to them, being upset because they taught the people and proclaimed in Jesus the resurrection from the dead. They laid hands on them, and put them in custody until the next day, for it was now evening. But many of those who heard the word believed, and the number of the men came to be about five thousand.
slang_text: So there's Peter and John, still rapping to the crowd, when the priests, the temple head honcho, and the Sadducees come rolling up totally bent out of shape. Way harsh -- these dudes were teaching the people and going on about Jesus rising from the dead, and that was major bogus news to the Sadducees. They grabbed 'em and tossed 'em in the slammer overnight 'cause it was already getting dark out. But no duh, tons of folks who heard the message believed anyway -- the crew of believers shot up to like five thousand dudes, for real.

web_text: In the morning, their rulers, elders, and scribes were gathered together in Jerusalem. Annas the high priest was there, with Caiaphas, John, Alexander, and as many as were relatives of the high priest. When they had stood Peter and John in the middle of them, they inquired, “By what power, or in what name, have you done this?” Then Peter, filled with the Holy Spirit, said to them, “You rulers of the people, and elders of Israel,
slang_text: Next morning, the big-wigs, the elders, and the scribes all posse up in Jerusalem. Annas the high priest is there, plus Caiaphas, John, Alexander, and basically the whole high priest family tree. They plant Peter and John right in the middle of the circle and start grilling 'em: "By what power, or whose name, did you dipsticks pull this off?" Then Peter, totally pumped up with the Holy Spirit, looks 'em dead in the eye and says, "Listen up, rulers of the people and elders of Israel --

web_text: if we are examined today concerning a good deed done to a crippled man, by what means this man has been healed, be it known to you all, and to all the people of Israel, that in the name of Jesus Christ of Nazareth, whom you crucified, whom God raised from the dead, in him does this man stand here before you whole. He is ‘the stone which was regarded as worthless by you, the builders, which has become the head of the corner.’ There is salvation in none other, for neither is there any other name under heaven, that is given among men, by which we must be saved!”
slang_text: -- if we're getting the third degree today over a righteous good deed done for a crippled dude, over how this guy got healed up, then get a clue, all of you, and every last person in Israel too: it's by the name of Jesus Christ of Nazareth, the guy you nailed to a cross, that God brought back from the dead -- it's because of him this man is standing here totally whole, right in front of your faces. He's 'the stone you builders tossed out as bogus, that ended up being the head cornerstone.' There's no salvation in anybody else, no way. There ain't another name under heaven given to us mortals that can save us -- this is it!"

web_text: Now when they saw the boldness of Peter and John, and had perceived that they were unlearned and ignorant men, they marveled. They recognized that they had been with Jesus. Seeing the man who was healed standing with them, they could say nothing against it. But when they had commanded them to go aside out of the council, they conferred among themselves, saying, “What shall we do to these men? Because indeed a notable miracle has been done through them, as can be plainly seen by all who dwell in Jerusalem, and we can’t deny it.
slang_text: Now when the council saw how bold Peter and John were being, and clocked that these were just regular joes with zero fancy schooling, they were totally floored. Then it clicked -- oh snap, these two had been rolling with Jesus. And with the healed dude standing right there next to 'em, plain as day, there was nothing bogus they could say against it. So they made Peter and John bounce outside for a sec while the council huddled up, going, "What are we even gonna do with these guys? 'Cause let's be real, a straight-up epic miracle went down through them -- everybody in Jerusalem's already seen it, and we can't deny it, no matter how hard we try.

web_text: But so that this spreads no further among the people, let’s threaten them, that from now on they don’t speak to anyone in this name.” They called them, and commanded them not to speak at all nor teach in the name of Jesus. But Peter and John answered them, “Whether it is right in the sight of God to listen to you rather than to God, judge for yourselves, for we can’t help telling the things which we saw and heard.”
slang_text: But so this doesn't spread any further among the people, let's lay down the law and threaten 'em good, so from now on they zip it about that name for good." So they called Peter and John back in and straight up ordered 'em not to talk or teach about Jesus ever again. But Peter and John shot back, "You be the judge -- is it even right in God's eyes to listen to you clowns instead of him? 'Cause there's no way we can keep quiet about what we've seen and heard. No way, no how."

web_text: When they had further threatened them, they let them go, finding no way to punish them, because of the people; for everyone glorified God for that which was done. For the man on whom this miracle of healing was performed was more than forty years old. Being let go, they came to their own company, and reported all that the chief priests and the elders had said to them.
